_directive: Prefer the canonical rendering of each term below for consistency, so translations and AI-generated suggestions slot into one vocabulary. BUT correctness wins: if a term has no natural equivalent in the target language, or forcing it would distort the meaning, render the meaning naturally instead. Better to lose the term than to mistranslate it. Never force. Keep proper nouns (ISO, WCAG, GDPR, welance) untranslated. Keep UI symbols (✓ ✗ ◐ ✦ ✚ ◀ ★ █) as-is.

terms.brief: the product brief handed to a team before a build — the artifact being scored
terms.the bar: the quality threshold a brief must clear; the rules + thresholds, collectively
terms.ruleset: the open set of rules; canonical in English
terms.verdict: the per-rule result: pass / partial / fail
terms.score: how good the brief is, 0-100 (a weighted average)
terms.gate: the separate set of hard requirements that must hold to publish at all; distinct from the score
terms.blocked: the gate failed — not published until the listed requirements are met
terms.with reservation: published but pending a human/community check
terms.Directory-ready: the top score band (>=85); publishable without reservation
terms.band: the descriptive label of a score: Directory-ready / Strong / Getting there / Needs work
terms.title: the brief's clear, specific heading
terms.scope: what is in vs out of the build
terms.deliverable: a concrete thing to be produced, with an acceptance condition (definition of done)
terms.budget floor: the minimum engagement budget policy (EUR 10k) a brief must clear
terms.timeline: a timeframe, deadline, or milestone for the work
terms.team shape: the roles, skills, or size of team the work calls for
terms.anonymised / blind-safe: the brief carries no brand, person, contact, or personal data, so teams judge substance not identity
terms.constraint: a technical bound: stack, platform, integration
terms.assumption: something taken as true that the build depends on
terms.compliance: legal regime for personal data of the product's users (GDPR, DPA) — distinct from anonymising the brief
terms.weight: how much a rule pulls the score
terms.severity: a rule's criticality label (metadata; the gate, not a cap, enforces hard requirements)
terms.fixture: a labelled example brief used as a regression test in CI
terms.add a fix: the action of inserting a suggested sentence into the brief
terms.tailor with AI: ask the LLM for suggestions specific to this brief
terms.grip on reality: the brief's honesty about problem, users, and measure
terms.doability: whether the brief is buildable as written
